- source chat:<0> [chat_turn role=USER observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: Memory MVP lesson for the NoMemoryVerifier component: Situation: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner can incorrectly return need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions. Action: run one short LLM verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. Result: we avoid brittle lexical hacks while still catching recall intent. Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.

structured.fullText: For ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, we recorded that NoMemoryVerifier should run a short, cheap model-based verification step before accepting a no-memory outcome instead of relying on brittle substring heuristics.
